scope: Pass B full-surface expansion against the real Eventzilla v2 developer docs (https://developer.eventzilla.net/docs/, https://www.eventzilla.net/api/). 6 read streams (events, categories, users, attendees, tickets, transactions -- the latter a per-event fan_out like attendees/tickets) and 2 write actions (checkin_attendee, toggle_event_sales) are covered. Excluded: single-resource detail GETs that duplicate an already-covered list stream's record shape (events/{id}, attendees/{attendeeid}, users/{userid}, transactions/{checkout_id or refno}); the multi-step registration/checkout flow (prepare, create, fillorder, confirm) and order confirm/cancel, which are compound, payment-carrying, multi-request workflows this dialect's single-request write shape cannot safely express and which mutate real customer orders/charges.

excluded.reason: single-event detail; identical record shape to the events list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
single-organizer detail; identical record shape to the users list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
single-attendee detail; identical record shape to the attendees list stream's per-item object (per-event fan_out), already covered by that stream
single-transaction detail keyed by checkout_id; identical record shape to the transactions stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
single-transaction detail keyed by order reference number instead of checkout_id; identical record shape to the transactions stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream

returns live payment-option/ticket-category/question/discount/tax configuration for an in-progress registration session; a preparatory read for the checkout flow below, not an independently syncable data stream

step 1 of the multi-step registration/checkout workflow (create -> fillorder -> confirm); a real customer-facing purchase-session creation that charges no money yet but reserves inventory, compound and stateful across 3 sequential requests this dialect's single-request write action cannot express
step 2 of the multi-step registration/checkout workflow; depends on the checkout_id returned by checkout/create, compound multi-request workflow out of scope for a single declarative write action

step 3 of the multi-step registration/checkout workflow; finalizes payment and books a real paid registration, compound multi-request workflow and a genuine financial transaction out of scope for this pass
confirms a pending order against a real payment/registration record on behalf of the buyer; a financially consequential state transition on someone else's order, not a plain data-object mutation
cancels an existing paid order/registration, an irreversible financial action affecting a real attendee's registration and refund eligibility
